name: Multi-Si panel; slanted-roof installation; on roof, 10 kWp
description:
This cradle-to-gate unit process dataset is for the production of 1 unit of 10 kWP slanted-roof mounted multi-Si panel system.
This unit process includes raw material acquisition; manufacturing; transport; production and use of electricity.

processDocumentation.timeDescription: The period spans from the publication date of the latest study used in Krebs et al. (2020) to their publication date, which is used as the "valid until" date, and of which only the month is known. Note that this leaves a validity period of less than a month.
processDocumentation.geographyDescription: The processes modeled in Krebs et al. (2020) are for a residential photovoltaic system in Switzerland.
processDocumentation.technologyDescription: This process in representative of the installation of a residential photovoltaic system in Switzerland for 2020. It includes panels, inverter, electric installation and roof mounting installation, as well as the transportation of these inputs, and the electricity required for their installation.
processDocumentation.modelingConstantsDescription: Krebs et al. (2020) assumed a life time of 30 years, for the mounting system, electric installation, and the overall installation, and 15 years for the inverters, which thus need to be replaced once. A distance of 100 km by ban and 600 km by truck was also assumed.
processDocumentation.completenessDescription:
Treatment of Missing Environmental Data
Krebs et al. (2020) offered no information on cutoff criteria or flows that might have not been considered.

Treatment of Missing Technosphere Data
Krebs et al. (2020) offered no information on cutoff criteria or flows that might have not been considered.

Mass Balance
No mass balance was calculated for this process.
processDocumentation.dataSelectionDescription: This unit process was modeled based on Krebs et al. (2020) inventory for a slanted-roof mounted 10 kWP multi-Si residential photovoltaic system available on Table 3.2 of said report.
processDocumentation.dataTreatmentDescription:
The values in the LCI in Krebs et al. (2020) reflect the expert judgement of the authors, since they do not seem a weighted average of the different studies.

Process data quality
Based on the information included in the “Data completeness section”, the data quality characteristic (DQC) "Process completeness" was given a score of 2 (60-79 % of determined flow have been evaluated and given a value). It is likely the original report went through a review process, but because it is not explicitly mentioned, the data quality characteristic "Process review" was left as default (No documented review) and thus with a score of 5."
processDocumentation.samplingDescription:
System boundaries
The system boundary is cradle to gate and includes direct emissions, material and energy use, and  transport. 

Data collection
The data that made Krebs et al. (2020) LCI is based on previous reports. See the original source for details.

Uncertainty
Krebs et al. (2020)  included a standard deviation for all flows, which is repeated here.
processDocumentation.dataCollectionDescription: The processes in Krebs et al. (2020) do not represent a real world residential system, but a modeled one. As such, there is no real collection period, and, as indicated in the Time description, the latest reference used in that report was published the same month as Krebs et al. (2020)

sources.name: Krebs et al. (2020) LCA residential PV

reviews.details: It is likely the original report went through a review process, but because it is not explicitly mentioned, the data quality characteristic "Process review" was left as default (No documented review) and thus with a score of 5.

processDocumentation.intendedApplication:
Situation C2 - Accounting, without system-external interactions - The intended application is a purely descriptive accounting / documentation of the analyzed system excluding interactions with other systems in the LCI model.
The target audience of this model are LCA practitioners, the silicon, solar and related industries and the general public. The inventory is considered largely complete, except for land transformation activities. For that reason, it is not recommended to use this dataset when evaluating land use impact categories. The results of the National Institute of Standards and Technology’s data gap analysis were analyzed using the ISO 21930-LCIA-US impact assessment methodology, as well as Cumulative Energy Demand and a subset of ReCiPe 2016 impact categories.
The original application of this dataset was the identification of data gaps in the Federal LCA Commons (FLCAC) (see ""Project"" above). However, the intended application is to provide a cradle-to-gate inventory (A1-A3, according to ISO 21930) using exclusively processes currently available in USLCI or generated as part of the project mentioned above, and elementary flows from the Federal Elementary Flow List (FEDEFL) to enable the use of a fuller range of LCIA impact categories than those reported in EPDs. For data quality purposes, the intended application is to provide a US average inventory for the year of publication of the original source.
processDocumentation.projectDescription: This dataset was developed as part of the “Public Life Cycle Inventory Data Gap Analysis for Silicon-based Residential Photovoltaic Systems” National Institute of Standards and Technology’s Technical Note.
